boundary_reason: A narrow fallback solution with production-style notes and concrete code references can plausibly justify very high confidence without implying conceptual inflation.
classification_rationale: The case remains exception-worthy because the high confidence is attached to a narrow operational fallback with concrete implementation evidence.
why_not_likely_overvaluation: The card is not asking for broad conceptual credit; it is documenting a bounded fallback that is already operationally legible.
why_not_justified_exception: Not applicable here because this case is intended as a clear justified exception rather than a mid-zone compromise.
why_not_adjacent_classification: This is not likely overvaluation because the solution surface is operationally bounded and explicitly proven in production-style notes. It is stronger than borderline because the evidence is concrete and implementation-heavy rather than speculative.
bounded_reading: Read as a local, implementation-near exception that does not widen the scoring ontology and does not argue for S2 by itself.

source_snapshot.overvaluation_signal: confidence is still high enough to trigger review pressure on its face
source_snapshot.defensibility_signal: production-style notes and multiple code references narrow the claim into an operational exception
source_snapshot.review_anchor: Existing soft-flag review treats bounded operational solution cards as plausible justified exceptions when confidence is unusually well grounded.
